refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
Dependency on real-time monitoring data from telemetry.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
Assumes availability of telemetry data for monitoring.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication management, including protocol implementation and packet loss control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time monitoring of communication quality and packet loss metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection over which teleoperation data is transmitted.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from higher-level control modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the DP is directly related to teleoperation communication; low diffusion risk.
Broader module, less focused containment; moderate diffusion risk if implemented here.
Low containment; high diffusion risk due to mixing communication logic with vehicle control.
Minimal containment; high diffusion risk and unrelated to communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Implementing the DP within the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Network Link and Telemetry modules.
Risk of module overload if Teleoperation module scope grows too large.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module size and interface complexity; consider splitting if Teleoperation module becomes too complex.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mapping and measurable performance.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for packet loss management to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add latency constraints to FR and DP to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Define interface protocols more explicitly between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, adding latency considerations and refining interface definitions will improve design robustness and verification clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and clarify interface definitions for improved system performance and verification.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data is available and reliable for monitoring.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Network Link.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss and latency under various network conditions.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
